events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes crane operations where outriggers are not fully extended on mats. This is a safety-critical issue. OSHA 1926.1400 requires outriggers to be fully extended and properly supported (on mats/cribbing) when required by the load chart. Not fully extending outriggers reduces the crane's stability and load capacity, potentially causing tipping. This is a clear non-compliance and safety hazard.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical (outriggers not fully extended can cause crane tip-over)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: estimate ~80% (rough-complete)
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading requested beyond the condition)
Confidence: high, say 0.9
Findings: outriggers not fully extended on mats
Actions: stop crane operations, fully extend outriggers onto proper mats/cribbing per load chart requirements, re-verify load chart capacity
Rationale: OSHA 1926.1400 requires outriggers fully extended and properly supported per manufacturer/load chart; partial extension reduces stability and capacity.
References: OSHA 1926.1400, load charts
